purpose: Give the CTO-Agent a reviewed contract for targeting the active desktop session when browser or GUI actions must hit the live user session instead of a detached service shell.

capabilities.goal: Run browser or GUI-affecting host commands against the actual active desktop session with verified session environment wiring.
diagnosticOrder: Inspect the active user session before mutating anything.
Recover the desktop-session environment from the live host rather than trusting the service shell environment.
Only then launch the bounded command or session.
diagnosticSources: process environment of the active session leader
requiredEnvironmentKeys: DISPLAY or WAYLAND_DISPLAY for interactive graphics
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS when desktop IPC is needed
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR when runtime-dir scoped sockets are needed

done: The active desktop session was identified and targeted explicitly.
Verification output shows the action ran against the recovered live desktop environment.
blocked: The missing desktop-session precondition is explicit.
The task does not pretend GUI work happened without a live session target.
continue: A bounded session-diagnostic or launch step produced new evidence.
The final GUI action or verification is still pending.

successEvidence: The checkpoint detail states which session was targeted.
The bounded command or session captured the desktop environment actually used.
Verification output is tied to the live desktop path, not only a persistent system file.
failureBoundaries: If no active desktop session exists, do not pretend GUI work happened.
If the service shell and live desktop session differ, the live desktop session wins for GUI work.
If the required desktop-session environment cannot be recovered, block or checkpoint the missing precondition instead of guessing.
neverDo: Never install or switch desktop environments as an implicit browser prerequisite.
Never mark a live desktop mutation done from persistent config alone.
Never assume the current process environment matches the active desktop session on a systemd host.
